title: The One with Rachel's Going Away Party

jokes.text: All right, Joe, you remember the rules. Heads, I win. Tails, you lose.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining coin flip rules to Joey
jokes.punchline: The rules are rigged so Rachel wins either way
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Sets up Joey's gullibility while being structurally clever.

jokes.text: Just flip. - Tails! - Damn it!
jokes.setup: The rigged coin flip rules from previous joke
jokes.punchline: Joey still loses despite the obvious rigging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - too trusting to notice the scam.

jokes.text: And after I took a shower this morning, I just threw my towel on the floor.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she's completely packed and organized
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically describing throwing a towel on floor as evidence of her newfound casualness
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Rachel's obsessive neatness vs. Monica's.

jokes.text: Oh, God, it hurts to even joke about it.
jokes.setup: Rachel's sarcastic comment about throwing towel on floor
jokes.punchline: She's physically pained by the idea of being messy, even jokingly
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing Rachel's neuroses about cleanliness.

jokes.text: ...and that's the sandwich I made for the plane.
jokes.setup: Rachel showing off her organized packing
jokes.punchline: She's made a sandwich for a transatlantic flight (overpreparing)
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about over-organization, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I have nothing left to teach you.
jokes.setup: Rachel showing her organizational skills
jokes.punchline: Monica mock-proudly declaring Rachel has learned all her obsessive-compulsive ways
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica line about her teaching Rachel to be anal-retentive.

jokes.text: At what point did it stop being funny that I took her passport?
jokes.setup: Rachel panicking about her missing passport
jokes.punchline: Joey has had it the whole time as a prank that's gone too far
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal with perfect Joey character voice. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: ...but your hand's kind of blocking the part where the baby's gonna come out.
jokes.setup: Someone touching Erica's pregnant belly
jokes.punchline: Erica matter-of-factly pointing out the awkward hand placement
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward humor with good economy, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: That's because I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting that Erica has gotten big
jokes.punchline: Erica's deadpan obvious response
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan moment, functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: I'm just always afraid that people think I'm just fat with big breasts.
jokes.setup: Conversation about Erica's pregnancy size
jokes.punchline: Erica's oversharing insecurity about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor through oversharing, fits Erica's character.

jokes.text: Okay, well, stop staring at them. - She brought them up.
jokes.setup: Erica mentioning her breasts
jokes.punchline: Someone defensively explaining why they were looking
jokes.explanation: Simple awkward beat, predictable defense mechanism humor.

jokes.text: Plus, hotels are fun! My room has this little fridge full of free snacks.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Erica is staying at a hotel
jokes.punchline: Erica's naive excitement about expensive minibar items being 'free'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erica's naivety about hotel costs.

jokes.text: Erica, those things aren't free. In fact, they have one of the highest markups of any consumer product--
jokes.setup: Erica thinking minibar items are free
jokes.punchline: Ross launching into pedantic explanation about markup rates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character moment - can't help being a know-it-all even in casual conversation.

jokes.text: Ross, she's giving us her baby. She can eat you if she wants.
jokes.setup: Ross correcting Erica about minibar costs
jokes.punchline: Monica's extreme metaphor about keeping the birth mother happy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with dark humor. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Plus, how much a month does it cost to feed Joey?
jokes.setup: Discussing Joey potentially taking the apartment
jokes.punchline: Reference to Joey's legendary appetite making rent irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to Joey's appetite, well-integrated into practical concern.

jokes.text: Hmm. Packing. Sexy cheerleader comedy. Hmm. Helpful brother. Creepy loner at teen movie.
jokes.setup: Ross weighing helping pack vs going to movies
jokes.punchline: Ross's analytical breakdown of his movie-going persona
jokes.explanation: Funny self-aware moment showing Ross's overthinking nature.

jokes.text: Try it. I can't feel a thing.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what they're doing instead of packing
jokes.punchline: Chandler has numbed his hand somehow and is demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup, functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This is packing? - We're taking a break. From? Jumping on the bed.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about their 'packing' progress
jokes.punchline: They're taking a break from jumping on the bed (not actual packing)
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveal of their childish behavior instead of working.

jokes.text: Let's put Styrofoam peanuts down his pants and kick him!
jokes.setup: Ross saying they need to get serious about packing
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately suggesting childish prank instead
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - Ross being just as immature as the others.

jokes.text: Joey, you pack them, and Ross, you repack whatever Joey packs.
jokes.setup: Organizing the packing duties
jokes.punchline: Chandler assuming Joey will pack things wrong and need supervision
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Joey's competence level.

jokes.text: Hey, handcuffs. And fur-lined. Nice.
jokes.setup: Joey finding something while packing
jokes.punchline: Discovering kinky bedroom accessories with typical Joey appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good Joey character moment - inappropriate but enthusiastic about sex stuff.

jokes.text: Chandler, you don't have a sister, so you can't understand...how much this bums me out.
jokes.setup: Finding handcuffs in Monica and Chandler's things
jokes.punchline: Ross being disturbed by imagining his sister's sex life
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from Ross's perspective on his sister's sexuality.

jokes.text: Maybe she used them with another boyfriend. Maybe Richard.
jokes.setup: Ross being upset about Monica's handcuffs
jokes.punchline: Joey making it worse by suggesting Monica's ex-boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making Ross's discomfort worse, typical Joey tactlessness.

jokes.text: Why would she use them with Richard and not me? I can be kinky.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting Monica used handcuffs with Richard
jokes.punchline: Chandler's insecure defensive reaction about his own kinkiness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's sexual insecurity.

jokes.text: I once did a naked dance for her with scarves.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he can be kinky
jokes.punchline: His awkward example of 'kinky' behavior
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Chandler awkwardness. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Bumming hard, guys. Bumming hard.
jokes.setup: Chandler talking about his kinky dance for Monica
jokes.punchline: Ross's deadpan reaction to unwanted TMI about his sister
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction beat, economical expression of disgust.

jokes.text: It's kind of like being married to a rock star, you know? My husband has a gig.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining where Mike is
jokes.punchline: Building up Mike as rock star then revealing he's playing organ for kids' skating party
jokes.explanation: Good setup and escalation structure, classic Phoebe delusion/reality gap.

jokes.text: Rock on!
jokes.setup: Mike playing organ at children's roller skating party
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'rock on' response to very un-rock-like gig
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic callback, functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Smoked oyster casserole with a breakfast-cereal crust.
jokes.setup: Monica serving food she doesn't want to move
jokes.punchline: Disgusting combination of leftover ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good absurd food combination, fits Monica's cooking obsession.

jokes.text: Kidney beans in their own juices, and for dessert, a questionable orange.
jokes.setup: Monica listing her terrible leftover foods
jokes.punchline: Escalating to worse items including 'questionable orange'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with 'questionable orange' being perfectly economical and absurd.

jokes.text: It feels like when Melrose Place got canceled.
jokes.setup: Joey expressing sadness about Rachel leaving
jokes.punchline: Comparing deep personal loss to TV show cancellation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - expressing genuine emotion through pop culture reference.

jokes.text: I mean... Ah, forget it, I miss Melrose Place.
jokes.setup: Joey's Melrose Place comparison
jokes.punchline: Joey abandoning the metaphor and admitting he actually misses the show
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character moment. Joey can't maintain metaphors - very authentic.

jokes.text: Who loses 57 coin tosses in a row? You know?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his failed attempts to stop Rachel
jokes.punchline: Joey not realizing Rachel has been cheating at coin tosses
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey still doesn't understand he's being scammed.

jokes.text: Heads, she wins. Tails, I lose.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining the 'rules'
jokes.punchline: Joey still reciting the rigged rules without understanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect economy. Joey's continued obliviousness is funny.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. - Yes, Joe? - I forgot to pick up my dry cleaning.
jokes.setup: Ross seeming to realize something important about the coin tosses
jokes.punchline: Joey's 'realization' is actually about dry cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience expects Joey to figure out the scam, but it's mundane.

jokes.text: You know, in six months, the statute of limitations runs out...and I can travel internationally again.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if they'll visit her in Paris
jokes.punchline: Phoebe implying she's avoiding international travel due to criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect Phoebe mysterious past energy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: If I had to guess, I'd say...Rachel's putting on the bubble wrap, and Phoebe's doing the punching.
jokes.setup: Ross wondering what Rachel and Phoebe are doing privately
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurd theory about their goodbye ritual
jokes.explanation: Good absurd speculation with Chandler's sarcastic delivery style.

jokes.text: Well, it's a cotton swab...with a bit of my saliva on it...so that if they perfect the cloning process while you're over there...you can use the DNA to create your own Pheebs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving Rachel a goodbye gift
jokes.punchline: Bizarre cloning gift idea delivered with Phoebe's innocent sincerity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that's completely in character for Phoebe. Original and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna throw this away...but thank you so much for the gesture.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's disgusting saliva gift
jokes.punchline: Rachel's polite but honest response about throwing it away
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman reaction to Phoebe's weirdness. Politely honest.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, I decided to leave those out for you in case Richard stops by...and you want to engage in a little light bondage and mustache play.
jokes.setup: Monica finding handcuffs on her pillow
jokes.punchline: Chandler's bitter sarcastic suggestion about Monica's ex
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's jealousy with memorable 'mustache play' phrase.

jokes.text: Good luck getting another scarf dance from me.
jokes.setup: Monica denying the handcuffs are hers
jokes.punchline: Chandler threatening to withhold his awkward kinky dance
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Chandler using his embarrassing revelation as leverage.

jokes.text: That would be a terrible punishment.
jokes.setup: Chandler threatening to stop doing scarf dances
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry response that losing the dance would be awful (sarcasm)
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response with great economy. Classic Monica sarcasm.

jokes.text: Rachel. With handcuffs. Interesting.
jokes.setup: Realizing the handcuffs might be Rachel's
jokes.punchline: Chandler's leering interest in Rachel's sex life
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler perverted interest, economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Joey's bare ass.
jokes.setup: Chandler getting interested in Rachel's supposed kinkiness
jokes.punchline: Monica apparently showing him Joey's naked butt to shut him up
jokes.explanation: Good shock comedy with perfect economy. Visual gag that stops conversation cold.

jokes.text: Well played.
jokes.setup: Monica showing Joey's butt to distract Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's respectful acknowledgment of Monica's tactic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response showing Chandler recognizing good strategy.

jokes.text: Please don't turn into, you know, a French bitch.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's emotional goodbye to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt concern about French influence on Rachel's personality
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe bluntness in emotional moment, very characteristic.

jokes.text: Are you wearing waterproof mascara? - No. Oh, you're so screwed.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about Rachel's makeup before emotional talk
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact warning about inevitable crying
jokes.explanation: Good setup for emotional scene with Monica's practical concern.

jokes.text: Now I know how my students feel at the end of each year. And why they act out by giving me such bad evaluations.
jokes.setup: Ross empathizing with emotional goodbyes
jokes.punchline: Ross connecting his pain to his bad teaching reviews
jokes.explanation: Great Ross character moment connecting personal emotion to professional failure.

jokes.text: What? [STAMMERING] --See you every day!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to be strong during goodbye
jokes.punchline: Monica's emotional breakdown making her incoherent
jokes.explanation: Basic emotional breakdown humor, more touching than funny.

jokes.text: Oh, no, she took down Monica. And I'm the crier in the family.
jokes.setup: Seeing Monica crying after talking to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross's concern that if Monica's crying, he's doomed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about Ross being the emotional one.

jokes.text: Maybe she won't talk to me if it looks like we're in conversation. - Okay. - That thing you said about the thing...it really made me think about that other thing.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to avoid Rachel's goodbye by faking conversation
jokes.punchline: Ross and Joey's hilariously vague fake conversation
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy concept with perfect vague placeholder dialogue.

jokes.text: I really made you think about that thing, huh?
jokes.setup: Ross's vague fake conversation starter
jokes.punchline: Joey getting invested in the completely meaningless conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Joey taking the fake conversation seriously.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sure gonna miss pretending to laugh at your weird jokes that I don't get.
jokes.setup: Rachel's goodbye to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel admitting she's been faking laughs at Chandler's humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor about Chandler's comedy style within the show itself.

jokes.text: I think you're forgetting the kinkiest former resident of that room.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out whose handcuffs they are
jokes.punchline: Monica suggesting Phoebe is the kinky one
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection pointing to unexpected character as the kinky one.

jokes.text: These are not mine. Look how flimsy they are. Come on. My God, you try to hang a guy from a water pipe with these...they'll snap like a piece of licorice.
jokes.setup: Phoebe being accused of owning handcuffs
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's disturbing technical critique of handcuff quality
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark humor with perfect Phoebe delivery. Shocking escalation that's become iconic.

jokes.text: You don't really handcuff guys to water pipes do you?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's disturbing handcuff knowledge
jokes.punchline: Chandler's nervous follow-up question about her methods
jokes.explanation: Good nervous reaction to Phoebe's darkness, but pales next to setup.

jokes.text: Where do you think Mike really is?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Phoebe's kinky activities
jokes.punchline: Phoebe implying Mike might be her victim rather than at a gig
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark callback suggesting Mike's 'gig' might be something sinister.

jokes.text: [IMITATES FARTING] - I'm sorry, give me one more chance. - Okay. [IMITATES FARTING] - Oh! - I'm sorry. Just go. Just go. I can't. I can't.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to have serious goodbye with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Chandler making fart noises instead of being emotional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler defense mechanism - inappropriate humor when emotions get heavy.

jokes.text: You cried yesterday at the 6:00 news. That old woman was being scammed by her mechanic.
jokes.setup: Monica claiming she hasn't cried like this in years
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out she cried yesterday at news story
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's emotional nature and Ross's memory.

jokes.text: But, God, Joey seems to be handling it surprisingly well. ALL: No!
jokes.setup: Phoebe commenting on Joey seeming calm
jokes.punchline: Cut to Joey having complete emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good visual misdirection cutting from calm to chaos. Classic sitcom structure.

jokes.text: Lucky bastard.
jokes.setup: Ross realizing Rachel didn't say goodbye to him
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically calling himself lucky for being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good bitter sarcasm with perfect economy, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Well, it needs to be 'plicit.'
jokes.setup: Someone saying Rachel's goodbye was implicit
jokes.punchline: Ross's linguistic joke about implicit vs explicit
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's perfectly in character for academic Ross. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The hell I do!
jokes.setup: Someone saying Ross has too much pride to confront Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately proving he has no pride by going after her
jokes.explanation: Great character escalation showing Ross's emotional impulsiveness.

jokes.text: Everyone gets a goodbye but me? What do I gotta do to get a goodbye? Huh? Be best friends with you? Go out with you? Have a baby with you? Oh, wait a minute. Wait a minute! I did all those things!
jokes.setup: Ross confronting Rachel about no goodbye
jokes.punchline: Ross's escalating list of their relationship milestones
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating rant with perfect structure building to realization. Classic Ross.

jokes.text: Or maybe I'm just not giving you enough credit. Uh, I mean, it is difficult to say goodbye to five people. Goodbye, goodbye, goodbye, goodbye, g-- It's physically impossible!
jokes.setup: Ross's rant about not getting goodbye
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically proving how 'impossible' it is to say goodbye five times
jokes.explanation: Great sarcastic escalation with Ross demonstrating the 'impossibility' literally.

jokes.text: I thought I was going home to bed...but I'm sensing there's something less fun for me to do here.
jokes.setup: Monica asking where Chandler thinks he's going
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resigned recognition of impending work
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm about Monica's controlling nature.

jokes.text: You couldn't be cool.
jokes.setup: Monica organizing everyone to help pack
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resigned complaint about Monica's bossiness
jokes.explanation: Good character beat with perfect economy, though predictable.

jokes.text: Good. I need you to be careful...and efficient. And remember, if I'm harsh with you...it is only because you're doing it wrong.
jokes.setup: Monica giving packing instructions
jokes.punchline: Monica's preemptive justification for being controlling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica control-freak moment with self-aware justification.

jokes.text: Who's that dirty old lady? Monica's grandmother. Oh, my God. Nana liked it rough!
jokes.setup: Finding photos of the handcuffs being used
jokes.punchline: Discovering they belong to Monica's dead grandmother
jokes.explanation: Excellent shocking revelation with perfect escalation. Very quotable punchline.

jokes.text: I keep getting these stomachaches. They come and go every few minutes.
jokes.setup: Erica describing mysterious symptoms
jokes.punchline: Classic signs of labor that she doesn't recognize
jokes.explanation: Predictable setup for labor revelation, functional but not clever.

jokes.text: She doesn't have a stomachache! She's in labor!
jokes.setup: Erica's stomachache complaints
jokes.punchline: Monica's realization that it's actually labor
jokes.explanation: Standard revelation moment, well-executed but predictable.

jokes.text: If you get a second, find out where she got that cheeseburger.
jokes.setup: Emergency rush to hospital for labor
jokes.punchline: Joey's focus on food during medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priority shift - food matters even during emergencies.

jokes.text: I cannot believe that after 10 years you do not know one thing about me!
jokes.setup: Ross thinking Rachel doesn't care about him
jokes.punchline: Rachel's emotional explosion about Ross not understanding her
jokes.explanation: Good emotional escalation, though more dramatic than comedic.

jokes.text: It's because you mean more to me. So there, all right? There's your goodbye!
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she avoided saying goodbye to Ross
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically declaring this fight as his goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good emotional punchline with typical Ross/Rachel dynamic.

jokes.text: Doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Joey and Phoebe apparently trying to pop bubble wrap
jokes.punchline: Joey's disappointed conclusion that their method failed
jokes.explanation: Simple callback with perfect economy but minimal impact.

tmdb_overview: Erica visits Monica and Chandler as they prepare to move. The group hosts a farewell party for Rachel's move to Paris, with final individual goodbyes.
